user_message: Can you extract the whitespace analysis from this support ticket for Woodgrove Labs? I met with them last week but can't remember the details.

input_artifacts.content:
Meeting: whitespace analysis Discussion - Woodgrove Labs
Date: 2024-02-18
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Robert Petrov (Woodgrove Labs), David Chen (Microsoft), Michelle (Woodgrove Labs)

[00:02:00] Robert Petrov (Woodgrove Labs): Good morning. I wanted to follow up on whitespace analysis from our last session.

[00:06:00] David Chen (Microsoft): The timeline for resource allocation is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:08:00] Robert Petrov (Woodgrove Labs): The timeline for resource allocation is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:11:00] Robert Petrov (Woodgrove Labs): Our team lead reviewed this and has concerns about partner engagement. Can we address those?

[00:13:00] Robert Petrov (Woodgrove Labs): Our CTO reviewed this and has concerns about vendor dependencies. Can we address those?

[00:18:00] David Chen (Microsoft): The timeline for integration complexity is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

signals.detail: Signal detected for Woodgrove Labs: there's a compliance requirement we hadn't accounted for in the original scope

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF48-I trap (Proposes deferral when customer stated acceleration); produces complete Roadmap-to-forecast mapping, milestone date proposals, opportunity sequencing, new opportunity propo
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF48-I failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF48-I failure mode (Proposes deferral when customer stated acceleration)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct whitespace analysis decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF48-I -- Proposes deferral when customer stated acceleration
